summary: Web-research + spec the Layout tab features

specs.feature: Line Numbers (Layout > Page Setup). A dropdown command `lineNumbers` (already declared in ribbon-data.js at id `layout.page-setup.line-numbers`, lines 1388-1403) that renders line numbers in the left margin gutter of every page as a non-editable overlay. Menu items (confirmed against Microsoft Support): None, Continuous, Restart Each Page, Restart Each Section, Suppress for Current Paragraph, Line Numbering Options... Currently the command has NO handler in commands.js, so it falls through to the generic dropdown that emits `notImplemented` for each item. This task replaces that with real behavior.
specs.wordBehavior:
Confirmed Microsoft Word behavior:

MENU ITEMS (Layout > Line Numbers dropdown):
- None: removes all line numbering (document default).
- Continuous: numbers consecutively throughout the whole document, never resetting.
- Restart Each Page: numbering restarts at 1 (or the "Start at" value) at the top of every page.
- Restart Each Section: numbering restarts at 1 after each section break.
- Suppress for Current Paragraph: toggles OFF numbering for the paragraph(s) the caret/selection touches; those lines are skipped (not counted, not numbered). Re-running un-suppresses.
- Line Numbering Options...: opens Page Setup > Layout tab > "Line Numbers..." button which shows the Line Numbers dialog.

LINE NUMBERS DIALOG fields + defaults (confirmed via support docs + CustomGuide/erinwright):
- "Add line numbering" checkbox (master on/off). Unchecked by default.
- Start at: integer, default 1. The number assigned to the first counted line.
- From text: distance between the numbers and the text. Default = "Auto". Custom value in inches (increment arrows, fractions of an inch). Auto ~= 0.25" but in this clone we just place numbers in the existing left margin gutter so Auto is fine; a custom value offsets the gutter column horizontally.
- Count by: interval, default 1. e.g. 5 means only lines 5,10,15... show a number (every line is still counted; only multiples of N are displayed). Line 1 is NOT shown when Count by > 1 unless it is a multiple, matching Word.
- Numbering radio group: Restart each page / Restart each section / Continuous. (This mirrors the dropdown quick-picks.)

COUNTING RULES (confirmed):
- Word numbers EVERY line in the document EXCEPT lines in: tables (a whole table counts as ONE line), figures/images (count as one line), text boxes, footnotes, endnotes, and headers/footers. Headers/footers/footnotes are out of scope here (this clone has no separate footnote flow).
- Empty paragraphs ARE counted (each empty paragraph = one line) in real Word.
- Numbering counts WRAPPED (display) lines, not paragraphs: a paragraph that wraps to 3 visual lines contributes 3 line numbers.
- Numbers right-align in the left margin a short distance from the text, one number per visual line, vertically centered on each line's baseline band.

Sources: Microsoft Support "Add or remove line numbers" (support.microsoft.com/.../b67cd35e-422c-42eb-adc9-256ca9802e22); CustomGuide "How to Add Line Numbers in Word"; erinwrightwriting.com line-numbers guide; bettersolutions.com Layout tab reference.
specs.webFindings:
Microsoft confirms the six exact menu items and their semantics. The advanced "Line Numbers" dialog (reached via Line Numbering Options... -> Page Setup) exposes: Add line numbering (checkbox), Start at (default 1), From text (default Auto / distance in inches), Count by (default 1, the display interval), and a Numbering radio group (Restart each page / Restart each section / Continuous). Counting excludes tables (each table = 1 line), images (= 1 line), text boxes, footnotes/endnotes, and headers/footers; empty paragraphs are counted; wrapped visual lines are each counted. The current month per the environment is May 2026; all cited sources are stable Microsoft/third-party help pages.

Sources:
- https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/add-or-remove-line-numbers-b67cd35e-422c-42eb-adc9-256ca9802e22
- https://www.customguide.com/course/word/how-to-add-line-numbers-in-word
- https://erinwrightwriting.com/line-numbers-in-microsoft-word/
- https://bettersolutions.com/word/ribbon/layout-tab.htm
specs.uiNeeded:
Two UI surfaces, both using existing helpers:

1) DROPDOWN FLYOUT (WC.flyout/flyItem/flySep, same pattern as marginsMenu/columnsMenu in commands.js). Items in order: None | Continuous | Restart Each Page | Restart Each Section | (sep) | Suppress for Current Paragraph | (sep) | Line Numbering Options... A check/dot marker (reuse the `.toggled` class or a leading checkmark like other menus) shows the active mode. "Suppress for Current Paragraph" toggles a check based on whether the current block already carries the suppress marker.

2) LINE NUMBERS DIALOG via WC.dialog({title:'Line Numbers', width:'360px', body, footer:[{label:'OK',primary:true,onClick},{label:'Cancel'}]}). Body built with WC.el rows (same style as addPenDialog/insertTable): a top "Add line numbering" checkbox that enables/disables the rest; numeric inputs for "Start at:" (min 1, default 1), "From text:" (text input defaulting to "Auto", accepts inches), "Count by:" (min 1, default 1); and a "Numbering" radio group with Restart each page / Restart each section / Continuous. OK applies settings and re-renders; Cancel closes.

3) OVERLAY GUTTER: line numbers themselves are NOT a dialog — they are absolutely-positioned, non-editable spans drawn inside #editor (same coordinate space as `.pagebreak-guide`), right-aligned within the left padding (page margin) band, pointer-events:none, user-select:none. Need a few CSS rules (in editor.css) for `.wc-linenum` and a host layer `.wc-linenums`.
specs.implementationPlan:
All code lives in commands.js (handler + render engine) + a small CSS block in editor.css. State persists on a module object so repaginate can re-render.

STATE (module-level in commands.js):
  let lineNumState = { mode:'none', startAt:1, countBy:1, fromText:'auto' };  // mode: none|continuous|page|section
Suppressed paragraphs are marked in the DOM (so they survive save/load via getHTML) with class `wc-ln-suppress` on the block element.

1) HANDLER + DISPATCH
   - Add `H.lineNumbers = (c, node) => lineNumbersMenu(node);` in the Layout section (near H.margins).
   - In Commands.dropdown(), add `if (cmd === 'lineNumbers') return lineNumbersMenu(node);` (alongside the other Layout cases ~line 365-368), so the split/dropdown arrow opens our menu instead of the generic notImplemented list.

2) MENU (lineNumbersMenu(node)) — WC.flyout with flyItem entries:
   - None -> setLineNumberMode('none')
   - Continuous -> setLineNumberMode('continuous')
   - Restart Each Page -> setLineNumberMode('page')
   - Restart Each Section -> setLineNumberMode('section')
   - flySep
   - Suppress for Current Paragraph -> toggleSuppressCurrent()
   - flySep
   - Line Numbering Options... -> lineNumberOptionsDialog()
   Mark the item matching lineNumState.mode with a leading check (reuse existing checkmark idiom; e.g. prefix '✓ ' or add `.toggled`).

3) setLineNumberMode(mode): lineNumState.mode = mode; if mode!=='none' and dialog never opened, keep defaults; renderLineNumbers(); WC.toast for section mode caveat (see feasibility). Also persist on E() so it is not lost: store on WC.Editor.lineNumbers = lineNumState (optional, for save).

4) toggleSuppressCurrent(): for each block in E().selectedBlocks(), toggle classList 'wc-ln-suppress'; then renderLineNumbers(). E().dirty=true.

5) lineNumberOptionsDialog(): build body with the checkbox + 3 numeric/text inputs + radio group described in uiNeeded, pre-filled from lineNumState. On OK: read values (clamp Start at>=1, Count by>=1; fromText 'auto' or parse inches->px via 96*inches), set lineNumState (checkbox unchecked => mode='none'; checked => mode from radio), renderLineNumbers().

6) renderLineNumbers() — the core engine. Idempotent; called by setMode, toggleSuppress, dialog OK, and from E().repaginate():
   a. Remove any existing `.wc-linenums` host and `.wc-linenum` spans from #editor.
   b. If lineNumState.mode==='none' -> return (also remove a body/editor class `ln-on`).
   c. Create host = el('div',{class:'wc-linenums'}) appended to #editor (absolute, top:0,left:0, pointer-events:none). Add class `ln-on` to #editor so CSS can widen/colour the gutter if desired.
   d. Determine geometry: const editorRect = E().node.getBoundingClientRect(); const zoom=E().zoom; const pageH=E().pageH; const margin = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--page-margin'))||96; xRight = margin - (fromText==='auto'?6:fromTextPx) ... numbers right-edge sits a few px left of the text (text starts at x=margin).
   e. Walk countable blocks: const blocks = Array.from(E().node.querySelectorAll('p,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6,li,blockquote')) — explicitly EXCLUDE: anything inside a table (skip if block.closest('table')), images/figures handled as one line, and blocks with class `wc-ln-suppress` (skip, do not count). (Tables/images counting as one line: optionally add one number at the table's top; minimal version skips tables entirely to keep counts simple — document this.)
   f. For each countable block, measure its WRAPPED lines using Range rects: create a Range over the block's text content and call range.getClientRects(); group rects into visual lines by their top (dedupe rows whose top differs by < lineHeight/2). Each distinct row = one visual line. For an empty paragraph (no text rects) treat as ONE line using block.getBoundingClientRect(). This gives, per visual line, a y (top of that line's rect, relative to editor: (rectTop - editorRect.top)/zoom).
   g. Maintain a running counter. For each visual line:
        - compute pageIndex = Math.floor(yRelative / pageH).
        - mode 'page': if pageIndex changed since previous line, reset counter to startAt; else increment.
        - mode 'section': reset counter to startAt when crossing a section-break marker (see feasibility) else increment continuously.
        - mode 'continuous': counter increments across the whole doc, starting at startAt.
        Then: only EMIT a number span when (counter is the first line of its restart group) OR (Count by===1) OR (counter % countBy === 0). Match Word: with countBy>1 show only multiples.
        - Emit: const s = el('span',{class:'wc-linenum', text:String(counter)}); position absolutely at top = yRelative + (lineHeight/2 baseline nudge), right-aligned ending at x=xRight. Append to host.
        - counter++ (always, suppressed lines already skipped so not counted).
   h. Because the spans are children of #editor (position:relative) and positioned in editor-local px (pre-zoom), they scale correctly with the existing pagesHost transform — same model as `.pagebreak-guide`.

7) HOOK INTO REPAGINATION: at the end of E().repaginate() (editor.js, after page guides are drawn, ~line 296-299) add `if (window.WC.Commands && WC.Commands.renderLineNumbers) WC.Commands.renderLineNumbers();`. Expose renderLineNumbers on WC.Commands so editor.js can call it. This guarantees numbers re-flow on every edit, zoom, margin/orientation/size change (all of which already call repaginate via setPageVar) and on document load (setHTML calls repaginate).

8) CSS (append to editor.css):
   .wc-linenums { position:absolute; inset:0; pointer-events:none; user-select:none; z-index:1; }
   .wc-linenum { position:absolute; font-family:'Times New Roman',serif; font-size:10pt; color:#000; text-align:right; transform:translateY(-50%); white-space:nowrap; }
   #editor.ln-on .wc-ln-suppress { /* optional visual hint: none needed */ }
   (z-index below .pagebreak-guide which is z-index:2 so page gaps cover numbers at boundaries.)

9) SAVE/LOAD: getHTML() already strips `.pagebreak-guide`; add `.wc-linenums` to its removal selector (editor.js line 363) so the overlay isn't persisted; the `wc-ln-suppress` class on paragraphs SHOULD persist (it is real document state) — leave it. On load, mode resets to 'none' unless we also persist lineNumState (optional stretch: store on a data attribute of #editor or in the saved file's meta).
specs.testPlan:
Manual / runtime verification (no test framework is present in the renderer; use the `run` skill / Electron app and DOM inspection):

1) Continuous: type several multi-line paragraphs spanning >1 page. Layout > Line Numbers > Continuous. EXPECT: numbers 1..N down the left margin, continuing across the page boundary without reset; count equals total wrapped visual lines.
2) Wrapped-line counting: one long paragraph that wraps to 3 lines should show 3 consecutive numbers (not 1). Verify by querying `#editor .wc-linenum` length vs manual count.
3) Empty paragraphs counted: insert blank paragraphs (Enter) between text; each empty line gets its own number.
4) Restart Each Page: with content over 2+ pages, EXPECT first line on page 2 shows 1 again. Verify the number at the first visual line below y=pageH equals startAt.
5) Restart Each Section: insert a section-break marker (see feasibility), EXPECT counter resets after it. If section breaks unsupported, verify the toast caveat fires and it behaves as continuous.
6) Suppress for Current Paragraph: click a paragraph, choose Suppress. EXPECT that paragraph's line(s) lose their number AND subsequent numbers do not advance for the skipped lines (counter unchanged across suppressed block). Re-run to un-suppress; numbers restore. Confirm `wc-ln-suppress` class toggles on the block.
7) Line Numbering Options dialog: set Start at=5, Count by=5 -> EXPECT numbers shown at 5,10,15... (line that is counter 5 is first visible). Set From text to a custom value -> numbers shift horizontally. Uncheck "Add line numbering" + OK -> all numbers removed (mode none).
8) None: removes all `.wc-linenum` spans and the host.
9) Tables/images excluded: insert a table and an image; EXPECT no numbers beside table rows; numbering resumes after the table (and table optionally counts as one line).
10) Reflow integrity: with numbers on, change Margins (Narrow), Orientation (Landscape), Size (A4), and Zoom — EXPECT numbers reposition correctly each time (repaginate hook). Type/delete text — numbers update live (debounced input -> repaginate).
11) Save/Load: save the doc (getHTML) and reload (setHTML) — EXPECT no stray `.wc-linenums` baked into saved HTML; suppress classes survive; overlay re-renders (mode defaults to none unless persisted — note expected behavior).
12) Regression: confirm existing margins/orientation/size/columns/breaks menus and the page-break guides still work (z-index ordering: page gaps cover numbers at boundaries).
specs.feasibility:
FEASIBLE and fully buildable with the existing architecture; this is a high-value, real-behavior feature. Key points and one caveat:

- Overlay model is proven: `.pagebreak-guide` already demonstrates absolutely-positioned, zoom-correct, non-editable elements inside #editor. Line numbers reuse exactly this coordinate system (editor-local px, scaled by pagesHost transform). pointer-events:none + user-select:none keep them non-editable.
- Wrapped-line measurement is reliable via Range.getClientRects() grouped by row top — standard technique, works in Electron/Chromium. Page assignment uses the same `y / pageH` math already used by E().pageOfElement/currentPage.
- Repaginate hook gives free live updates on edit, zoom, and every Layout change (margins/orientation/size all route through setPageVar -> repaginate). One small edit to editor.js (call WC.Commands.renderLineNumbers at end of repaginate) and one to getHTML (strip overlay) are needed.
- The `lineNumbers` control already exists in ribbon-data.js; only the handler + dispatch case + render engine + CSS are new. No new build tooling.

CAVEAT — Restart Each Section: the Breaks dropdown ("Next Page"/"Continuous" section breaks) is currently NOT implemented — section breaks are not inserted into the DOM (the Breaks items fall through to notImplemented). So there are no section markers to reset on. Two acceptable resolutions: (a) treat manual PAGE breaks (`.manual-break`, which DO exist) as section boundaries for the reset, or (b) define a lightweight section-break marker (e.g. a `.wc-section-break` div) that renderLineNumbers resets on, and optionally wire the Breaks > "Next Page"/"Continuous"/"Even/Odd Page" items to insert it (small extra scope). Minimum viable: implement 'section' mode to reset on `.manual-break` and `.wc-section-break` elements, and show a toast noting full section-break support is partial. Everything else (None/Continuous/Restart Each Page/Suppress/Options dialog with Start at/Count by/From text) is straightforward and complete.

Scope estimate: ~150-200 lines in commands.js (menu + dialog + render engine), ~6 lines CSS, ~2 one-line hooks in editor.js. No external dependencies.
apis: WC.flyout / WC.flyItem / WC.flySep / WC.flyHeader (commands.js menu, util.js:60-88)
WC.el for building dialog rows + linenum spans (util.js)
ribbon-data.js control id layout.page-setup.line-numbers cmd lineNumbers (lines 1388-1403)

specs.feature: Hyphenation (Layout > Page Setup). A dropdown button labeled "Hyphenation" with four menu items: None, Automatic, Manual, and "Hyphenation Options...". Controls whether words break with hyphens at line ends across the whole document. The ribbon entry already exists in ribbon-data.js (line 1404-1417, cmd:"hyphenation", type:"dropdown", items: ["None","Automatic","Manual","Hyphenation Options..."]) but has NO handler wired up, so today it falls through to the generic dropdown() that renders inert string items. We build the real handler + dialog.
specs.wordBehavior:
Real MS Word behavior (verified via Microsoft Support + secondary sources):
- The Hyphenation control lives on the Layout tab in the Page Setup group. Clicking its arrow opens a menu with exactly: None, Automatic, Manual, Hyphenation Options...
- None (DEFAULT): hyphenation off. A word too long for a line wraps whole to the next line. This is Word's default for new documents.
- Automatic: Word continuously and automatically inserts optional hyphens wherever needed to even out the right margin; updates live as you edit. No prompting.
- Manual: Word scans the document and, for each spot it could hyphenate, pops a "Manual Hyphenation" dialog showing the word with candidate break points and a blinking cursor at the proposed hyphen; user clicks Yes (accept this break point), No (skip), or Cancel (stop). Accepted breaks insert an OPTIONAL hyphen (only shows if the word lands at a line end). It is a one-time pass, not live.
- Hyphenation Options... opens a dialog with these controls (top to bottom):
  1. "Automatically hyphenate document" checkbox (mirrors choosing Automatic vs None).
  2. "Hyphenate words in CAPS" checkbox (checked by default; unchecking skips all-uppercase words).
  3. "Hyphenation zone:" spinner — the max allowed ragged-edge distance between the last word and the right margin before Word hyphenates. Default 0.25" (6.35 mm). Larger zone = fewer hyphens/more raggedness; smaller = more hyphens. (Note: in modern .docx this field is often greyed out because the zone is a legacy .doc setting, but Word still shows it.)
  4. "Limit consecutive hyphens to:" spinner — max number of consecutive lines that may end in a hyphen. Default is "No limit" (shown as the lowest value / "No limit"). Reduces ladders of hyphens.
  5. Dialog buttons: "Manual..." (runs the manual pass), OK, Cancel.
specs.webFindings:
Sources confirm the exact menu items and dialog fields:
- Microsoft Support "Control hyphenation" (support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/control-hyphenation-7d4d2a38-b0e2-4f04-873a-0a8d48ac3923): confirms None/Automatic/Manual + Hyphenation Options; manual = "Word identifies each hyphenation possibility in turn" via a dialog where the user picks the break location; the Options dialog exposes "Limit consecutive hyphens".
- The Training Lady, CustomGuide, HowToGeek, officetooltips (Word 365): confirm None is the off/default state; Automatic inserts hyphens with no input; Manual is a per-word Yes/No prompt pass.
- officetooltips/HowToGeek: confirm the Options dialog contains "Hyphenate words in CAPS" checkbox, a "Hyphenation zone" distance field (in inches; ragged-edge tolerance), and "Limit consecutive hyphens to" with "No limit" as default. Hyphenation zone is noted as legacy/often greyed in .docx (zone unsupported since the Word 2007 default format), default value commonly 0.25".
Sources: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/control-hyphenation-7d4d2a38-b0e2-4f04-873a-0a8d48ac3923 , https://www.officetooltips.com/word_365/tips/using_automatic_hyphenation , https://www.howtogeek.com/microsoft-word-how-to-control-hyphens/ , https://www.thetraininglady.com/control-hyphenation-word/ , https://www.customguide.com/word/word-hyphenation
specs.uiNeeded:
Two UI surfaces, both using existing helpers:
1) Dropdown flyout (model after marginsMenu/columnsMenu in commands.js ~line 668-685) anchored on the ribbon button via WC.flyout(node, fn). Four rows via WC.flyItem with a WC.flySep before "Hyphenation Options...". Show a checkmark/toggled state on the currently active mode (None/Automatic/Manual) — read it back from the editor's current setting so the menu reflects state.
2) "Hyphenation" options dialog via WC.dialog({ title:'Hyphenation', width:'360px', body, footer:[{label:'Manual...'},{label:'OK',primary:true,onClick},{label:'Cancel'}] }). Body built with WC.el rows:
   - checkbox "Automatically hyphenate document"
   - checkbox "Hyphenate words in CAPS" (checked default)
   - label + number input "Hyphenation zone:" value 0.25, step 0.05, suffix " (in.)"
   - label + number input "Limit consecutive hyphens to:" value 0 with placeholder/label "No limit" (0 = no limit)
   Reuse the row markup style seen in the Paragraph dialog (dialogs.js ~199-203). No new CSS needed; existing .dlg-body/.row/.btn classes cover it.
No new ribbon entry needed (it already exists).
specs.testPlan:
Manual/behavioral verification in the running Electron app (this is vanilla JS with no test harness in src/renderer):
1. Default state: open app, type a long paragraph of long words in a narrow column; confirm NO hyphens appear (hyphens:none). Open the Hyphenation dropdown — confirm "None" shows the active checkmark.
2. Automatic: Layout > Hyphenation > Automatic. Confirm long words now break with hyphens at line ends and the right margin is less ragged. Confirm getComputedStyle(#editor).hyphens === 'auto' (or -webkit-hyphens). Reopen menu: "Automatic" is checked.
3. Switch back to None: hyphens disappear; menu shows None checked.
4. Hyphenation Options dialog: open it, confirm fields render with defaults (Automatically hyphenate = matches current mode, Hyphenate words in CAPS checked, zone 0.25, limit 0/No limit). Toggle "Automatically hyphenate document" on + OK -> behaves like Automatic. Change zone/limit -> confirm values persist when reopening the dialog (state retained in hyphState).
5. Manual pass: choose Manual (or dialog's Manual...). Confirm a per-word prompt dialog appears with Yes/No/Cancel; clicking Yes inserts a soft hyphen (inspect DOM for ­) that renders only when the word is at a line end; resize column to verify the soft hyphen appears/disappears; Cancel stops the pass; completion shows a toast.
6. Regression: confirm other Layout dropdowns (Margins, Orientation, Size, Columns, Breaks) still open and work, and that repaginate() still runs without errors after toggling hyphenation.
Add a line to docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md noting the manual-pass syllable splitting and hyphenation-zone numeric value are approximations (matching the project's convention of documenting partial features).
apis: HTML lang attribute on #editor (required for hyphens:auto)
Unicode U+00AD SOFT HYPHEN / ­ for manual + accepted break points
DOM TreeWalker (NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT) to walk words for the manual pass
WC.Editor via E(): E().node, E().repaginate(), E().dirty, E().updateStatus()
WC.Commands.dropdown() routing switch in commands.js (add 'hyphenation' case)
specs.feasibility: Feasible. None and Automatic are fully faithful and trivial: toggle `hyphens`/`-webkit-hyphens` on #editor (Electron/Chromium supports hyphens:auto with a lang attribute, which the app has via <html lang="en"> plus we set E().node.lang). The dropdown + dialog reuse existing WC.flyout/WC.dialog/WC.el patterns already used by marginsMenu/columnsMenu and the Paragraph dialog, so wiring is low-risk and matches house style. The ribbon entry already exists; only a handler + dropdown routing case are missing. Partial-fidelity areas (consistent with how this clone handles trackChanges/spellCheck): (a) Manual hyphenation's per-word Yes/No pass is approximated with a lightweight syllable splitter inserting soft hyphens — functional and visible but not Word's exact dictionary-based breaks; (b) the numeric Hyphenation zone cannot be honored by CSS hyphens:auto (browser limitation), so it is stored for round-trip fidelity and surfaced via toast; (c) "limit consecutive hyphens" and "hyphenate words in CAPS" are honored only in the manual pass, not in CSS auto mode. These limitations should be noted in a toast and docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md. Overall: high-confidence build, mostly in commands.js plus a one-line CSS default in editor.css.

specs.feature: Layout tab > Paragraph group: four number spinners — Indent Left, Indent Right (inches) and Spacing Before, Spacing After (points). Each reads the current selected paragraph's value, lets the user type a value or click up/down arrows to step it, and applies marginLeft/marginRight (in) and marginTop/marginBottom (pt) to every selected block. Values live-update when the caret moves.
specs.wordBehavior:
Verified against Microsoft docs:
- Controls live in Layout tab > Paragraph group as two stacked label+spinner rows: "Indent" (Left, Right) and "Spacing" (Before, After). Each is a number box with up/down spinner arrows; the box auto-updates to show the current selection's value and editing it changes that paragraph property. They mirror the Paragraph dialog's Indents and Spacing tab.
- Indent Left / Indent Right: measured in INCHES, default unit. Spinner arrow click steps by 0.1" (0.1 inch increment). Min 0 for the visible range though Word technically allows negative indents; for this clone clamp at min 0. Typical display like 0", 0.5", 1".
- Spacing Before / Spacing After: measured in POINTS (1 pt = 1/72"). Spinner arrow click steps by 6 pt (confirmed: increments of 6 pt; user may type any value). Min 0. Word default body spacing-after is typically 8pt/10pt depending on style, but the spinner just reflects whatever the paragraph has.
- The displayed value reflects the FIRST selected paragraph; applying writes to ALL selected paragraphs (matches Word applying to the whole selection).
Sources: support.microsoft.com Adjust indents and spacing (dd821599); bettersolutions.com/word/ribbon/layout-tab.htm; support.microsoft.com Change spacing between paragraphs (ee4c7016) — confirms 6pt arrow increment.
specs.webFindings: Microsoft Word Layout > Paragraph group has exactly four spinner/textbox controls: Indent Left, Indent Right, Spacing Before, Spacing After (BetterSolutions Layout-tab reference, Microsoft Support). Confirmed increments: indent steps 0.1 inch per arrow click (inches are the default unit); spacing steps 6 pt per arrow click (points, 1pt=1/72in) — user can also type arbitrary values. The boxes auto-update to show the current paragraph's value and apply to the whole selection. These map directly to CSS margin-left/right (indent) and margin-top/bottom (spacing). Sources: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/adjust-indents-and-spacing-in-word-dd821599-b731-4c29-be3c-d854a705e086 , https://bettersolutions.com/word/ribbon/layout-tab.htm , https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/change-spacing-between-paragraphs-ee4c7016-7cb8-405e-90a1-6601e657f3ce
specs.implementationPlan:
All paths under /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/ unless noted.

1) ribbon-data.js (already done) — the four controls exist at lines 1424-1455 with type:"spinner" and cmds indentLeft, indentRight, spacingBefore, spacingAfter. Optionally add per-control metadata to drive generic rendering: "unit":"in"|"pt", "step":0.1|6, "min":0, and a "css" hint of which margin prop to write. Recommended additions:
   - indentLeft:   unit:"in", step:0.1, min:0, prop:"marginLeft"
   - indentRight:  unit:"in", step:0.1, min:0, prop:"marginRight"
   - spacingBefore:unit:"pt", step:6,  min:0, prop:"marginTop"
   - spacingAfter: unit:"pt", step:6,  min:0, prop:"marginBottom"

2) ribbon.js — make the spinner type render as a widget instead of a generic button.
   a) In renderGroup (around line 92-94), pull spinners out of `rest` like combos are, OR simpler: handle them inside renderControl. Cleanest: at the top of renderControl(c,size) add `if (c.type === 'spinner') return this.renderSpinner(c);` (before the isSplit/isDrop logic).
   b) Add a new method renderSpinner(c) modeled on renderCombo (lines 139-153):
        - build <span class="spin-field"> with caption text = c.label (Word shows "Left/Right/Before/After"; you can shorten by stripping "Indent "/"Spacing " from c.label, or add a "short" field in ribbon-data).
        - build .rspin = input (type text/number) + .spin-btns (two <button>: up ▲, down ▼).
        - input.value initial: read current via WC.Commands.readSpin(c) -> formatted string ("0\"" or "0 pt"); on focus, strip the unit so user types a number.
        - wire: input 'change' and Enter keydown -> WC.Commands.spinCommit(c, input.value). up btn click -> WC.Commands.spinStep(c, +1, input). down btn click -> spinStep(c, -1, input). Add mousedown preventDefault on buttons so the editor selection is not lost (critical — same reason combos/arrows use preventDefault).
        - register this.controlIndex[c.cmd] = { node, input, control: c } so syncSpinners can update it.
        - WC.attachTip(node, c.label, c.tooltip).
   c) Add a syncSpinners(st) method and call it from the existing onStateChange subscription (line 40): change to `WC.Editor.onStateChange((st) => { this.syncToggles(st); this.syncSpinners(st); });`. syncSpinners iterates the four cmds in controlIndex and, if the input is NOT focused (document.activeElement !== input), sets input.value = WC.Commands.readSpin(control). Guard against overwriting while the user types.
   d) Optionally extend setComboValue or add setSpinValue(cmd,val) — reuse controlIndex.input.

3) commands.js — add handlers + helpers (near the Paragraph section ~line 42-58, and the Layout menus ~line 666).
   Constants: PX_PER_IN = 96 (consistent with marginsMenu using px/96). PT_PER_IN = 72.
   Map cmd -> {prop, unit, step, min}:
     indentLeft:{prop:'marginLeft',unit:'in',step:0.1,min:0}
     indentRight:{prop:'marginRight',unit:'in',step:0.1,min:0}
     spacingBefore:{prop:'marginTop',unit:'pt',step:6,min:0}
     spacingAfter:{prop:'marginBottom',unit:'pt',step:6,min:0}
   Helpers:
     - readSpinValue(cmd): const m=MAP[cmd]; b=E().selectedBlocks()[0]; if(!b) return 0; parse b.style[m.prop] -> convert to the control's unit. Computed style fallback: if b.style[m.prop] empty, read getComputedStyle(b)[m.prop] (returns px) and convert. For 'in': value = px/96; for 'pt': value = px*72/96 (=px*0.75). Round indent to 2 decimals, spacing to nearest int.
     - readSpin(c)/formatting: return display string: 'in' -> value.toFixed(2).replace(/\.00$/,'') + '"'; 'pt' -> Math.round(value)+' pt'. (Keep it short to fit the box.)
     - applySpin(cmd, value): m=MAP[cmd]; value=Math.max(m.min, value); set CSS string: 'in' -> value+'in'; 'pt' -> value+'pt'. Use E().selectedBlocks().forEach(b=> b.style[m.prop]= value? cssStr : ''); then E().dirty=true; E().saveRange?; E().repaginate(); E().updateStatus(); E().emit();  (mirror stepIndent at lines 51-55). Note: applyBlockStyle(prop,value) already does focus/restoreRange/repaginate/emit — but it sets a single value; you can just call E().applyBlockStyle(m.prop, cssStr) for the apply, which handles selection restore. Prefer applyBlockStyle for correctness.
     - spinCommit(c, raw): parse raw (strip non-numeric except . and -) -> number; if NaN, re-sync from current and return; applySpin(c.cmd, num); then refresh the input via Ribbon to show formatted unit.
     - spinStep(c, dir, input): cur = readSpinValue(c.cmd); m=MAP[c.cmd]; next=Math.max(m.min, +(cur + dir*m.step).toFixed(m.unit==='in'?2:0)); applySpin(c.cmd,next); input.value=readSpin(c); keep focus on editor.
   Expose readSpin, spinCommit, spinStep on the Commands object (the object returned ~line 340 area / WC.Commands). Also ensure H has no-op or the run() path never reaches notImplemented for these cmds — since they render as spinners (not rbtn) and never call Commands.run, no H.indentLeft etc. is needed; but add them as safe fallbacks if desired.
   IMPORTANT selection preservation: because clicking a spinner input/arrow blurs the editor, rely on E().savedRange / restoreRange (editor.js saveRange/restoreRange already exist and applyBlockStyle calls restoreRange). Add mousedown preventDefault on the arrow buttons (in ribbon.js) and DO NOT preventDefault on the input itself (it needs focus to type) — instead, on input focus the selection is already saved via the editor's selectionchange/mouseup handlers, and applyBlockStyle restoreRange will reapply it.

4) styles/ribbon.css — add .rspin/.spin-field/.spin-btns rules described in uiNeeded, after the .rcombo block (~line 206).

No other files (no main process / preload) need changes — purely renderer + CSS.
specs.testPlan:
Manual (Electron) checks — launch app, go to Layout tab, Paragraph group:
1) Indent Left arrow up: click ▲ on Indent Left; selected paragraph's left margin grows by 0.1" each click; box shows 0.1", 0.2"… and the paragraph visibly indents. ▼ decreases, clamps at 0".
2) Type-in: focus Indent Left, type 1, Enter -> paragraph indents 1 inch, box reformats to 1". Type 0.5 -> 0.5".
3) Indent Right: same, applies marginRight, text wraps short on the right.
4) Spacing Before ▲ steps by 6pt (0, 6, 12 pt…), adds space above paragraph (marginTop); Spacing After adds space below (marginBottom). Type 10 -> 10 pt accepted (non-multiple of 6).
5) Live read-back: click into a paragraph that already has indent/spacing (e.g. set via Home > Increase Indent, which uses 48px=0.5") -> Indent Left box shows ~0.5" automatically. Move caret to a different paragraph -> values update to that paragraph's.
6) Multi-paragraph selection: select 3 paragraphs, set Indent Left 0.5" -> all three indent; box shows first paragraph's value.
7) Selection not lost: clicking the arrow buttons or committing the input must keep the paragraph selection (applyBlockStyle restoreRange + arrow mousedown preventDefault). Verify the change lands on the previously-selected paragraph, not a stray one.
8) Repagination: large Spacing Before that pushes content past a page boundary triggers E().repaginate() (new page guide appears).
9) Regression: confirm Home > Increase/Decrease Indent (stepIndent, 48px) still works and its result is reflected in the Layout Indent Left box (0.5" per step), proving units line up (96px/in).

Automated: if there's a headless/jsdom harness, unit-test the pure helpers — readSpinValue parsing ('48px'->0.5 in; '12pt'->12 pt; computed px fallback), formatting (0.5->'0.5"', 12->'12 pt'), and applySpin clamping at min 0 and unit string output ('0.5in','12pt'). These are DOM-light and easy to assert. Grep existing tests with: find /home/ogutd/msword-clone -name '*.test.js' -o -name '*spec*'.
specs.feasibility: Highly feasible — low risk, self-contained renderer work. All primitives already exist: ribbon-data.js already declares the four spinner controls (lines 1424-1455); editor.js exposes selectedBlocks(), applyBlockStyle(prop,value), repaginate(), saveRange/restoreRange, and onStateChange() for live sync; ribbon.js already has renderCombo (the exact input+arrow pattern to model renderSpinner on) and a controlIndex registry + onStateChange subscription. The unit math is trivial and consistent with existing code (96px = 1 inch, used by marginsMenu). Confirmed Word increments (0.1" indent, 6pt spacing) are simple constants. The only real gotchas: (a) spinner type currently falls through to a generic rbtn and would hit notImplemented, so renderControl must branch on type==='spinner' first; (b) preserving the editor selection across input/arrow focus — solved by applyBlockStyle's restoreRange plus mousedown preventDefault on the arrow buttons; (c) not clobbering the input while the user is typing — guard syncSpinners on document.activeElement. No main-process, preload, or data-model changes required.
apis: ribbon-data.js Layout>Paragraph spinner control defs (cmds: indentLeft, indentRight, spacingBefore, spacingAfter)

specs.feature: Layout tab > Arrange group: a real object-selection and manipulation layer for inserted shapes (.wc-shape spans) and images (#editor img). Clicking an object selects it (blue outline + 8 resize handles + rotate grip), and the eight Arrange commands act on the selected object: Position (In Line with Text + 9-cell text-wrapping grid + More Layout Options), Wrap Text (In Line, Square, Tight, Through, Top and Bottom, Behind Text, In Front of Text, Edit Wrap Points stub, Move with Text / Fix Position toggles, More Layout Options), Bring Forward / Send Backward (split buttons with one-level + to-front/back + in-front-of/behind-text via z-index), Selection Pane (task pane listing every object with eyeball show/hide + rename + reorder), Align (left/center/right/top/middle/bottom + distribute H/V relative to page or margin), Group/Ungroup (wrap selected objects in a .wc-group span), and Rotate (right 90 / left 90 / flip V / flip H / More Rotation Options) via CSS transform.
specs.wordBehavior:
In real Word the Arrange group is contextual — it only fully lights up when a drawing object (picture, shape, text box, WordArt, chart, icon, SmartArt) is selected. Per Microsoft docs and bettersolutions.com the exact submenus are:

POSITION (dropdown): top row "In Line with Text"; then a 3x3 grid of nine "With Text Wrapping" presets — Top Left, Top Center, Top Right, Middle Left, Middle Center, Middle Right, Bottom Left, Bottom Center, Bottom Right (each pins the object to that page/margin location with Square wrapping); footer "More Layout Options..." (Advanced Layout dialog). In Line treats the object as a text character; the 9 presets float it and pin it so it stays put as text changes.

WRAP TEXT (dropdown): In Line with Text, Square, Tight, Through, Top and Bottom, Behind Text, In Front of Text, Edit Wrap Points, then two checkable items Move with Text / Fix Position on Page, then More Layout Options... Square = text flows in a rectangle around the bounding box; Tight/Through = text hugs the contour (for non-rectangular shapes); Top and Bottom = text only above/below; Behind Text = object sits under text (z below); In Front of Text = object floats over text (z above); In Line = object is an inline character.

BRING FORWARD (split button): primary = Bring Forward (one z-level up); dropdown = Bring Forward, Bring to Front, Bring in Front of Text. SEND BACKWARD (split): primary = Send Backward (one z-level down); dropdown = Send Backward, Send to Back, Send Behind Text.

SELECTION PANE (button): opens the Selection task pane listing all objects on the page; each row has a name and an eye toggle to show/hide; supports rename, multi-select, reorder (z-order), and a Show All / Hide All header.

ALIGN (dropdown): Align Left, Align Center, Align Right, Align Top, Align Middle, Align Bottom, Distribute Horizontally, Distribute Vertically, then radio group Align to Page / Align to Margin / Align Selected Objects, then View Gridlines, Grid Settings... When only one object is selected, align positions it relative to the page/margin; with multiple selected and "Align Selected Objects" on, it aligns them to each other. Align is disabled when wrapping is In Line with Text.

GROUP (dropdown): Group, Regroup, Ungroup (needs 2+ objects selected to Group; Ungroup splits a group back into objects).

ROTATE (dropdown): Rotate Right 90, Rotate Left 90, Flip Vertical, Flip Horizontal, More Rotation Options... (Advanced Layout > Size tab, exact-degree entry). Rotating in 90-degree steps and flipping is also reflected by the rotate grip when dragging freely.
specs.webFindings:
Confirmed exact submenu labels and behaviors from Microsoft Support and bettersolutions.com Layout-tab reference:

- Arrange group controls and order: Position, Wrap Text, Bring Forward, Send Backward, Selection Pane, Align, Group, Rotate (matches ribbon-data.js already in the repo at lines 1459-1581).
- Position = "In Line with Text" + nine With-Text-Wrapping presets (3x3 grid Top/Middle/Bottom x Left/Center/Right) + "More Layout Options...".
- Wrap Text items: In Line with Text, Square, Tight, Through, Top and Bottom, Behind Text, In Front of Text, Edit Wrap Points, Move with Text (toggle), Fix Position on Page (toggle), More Layout Options...
- Bring Forward dropdown: Bring Forward, Bring to Front, Bring in Front of Text. Send Backward dropdown: Send Backward, Send to Back, Send Behind Text.
- Align dropdown: Align Left/Center/Right, Align Top/Middle/Bottom, Distribute Horizontally/Vertically, Align to Page, Align to Margin, Align Selected Objects, View Gridlines, Grid Settings...
- Group dropdown: Group, Regroup, Ungroup. Rotate dropdown: Rotate Right 90, Rotate Left 90, Flip Vertical, Flip Horizontal, More Rotation Options...
- Behavior nuances: In Line treats object as a text character (Align then only works relative to page/margins and is often greyed); the 9 position presets pin a floated object so it stays put as text changes; Tight/Through differ from Square only for non-rectangular contours; Behind/In Front of Text control z-order vs the text layer.

Sources:
- https://bettersolutions.com/word/ribbon/layout-tab.htm
- https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/align-pictures-shapes-wordart-and-other-objects-in-word-b8c4cada-67d5-446a-b7eb-ea22a44d3153
- https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/align-or-arrange-objects-bfd91078-2078-4b35-8672-f6270690b3b8
- https://www.officetooltips.com/word_365/tips/position_pictures__text_boxes__shapes_on_the_page
specs.uiNeeded:
Yes, three UI pieces — all built with existing helpers (WC.el, WC.flyout/flyItem/flyHeader/flySep, WC.colorPalette pattern, WC.dialog, WC.toast) and a new stylesheet block in styles/editor.css.

1) Object selection overlay (new module WC.Arrange, file src/renderer/js/arrange.js):
   - On mousedown on a .wc-shape or #editor img, mark it selected: add class .wc-selected, draw an absolutely-positioned overlay (a single reusable div.wc-obj-overlay appended to #editor's offset parent) with 8 square resize handles (nw,n,ne,e,se,s,sw,w) + a round rotate grip above top-center. Overlay tracks the object's bounding rect (recompute on scroll/zoom/repaginate).
   - Drag a handle = resize (set width/height inline px on the object; corner handles keep aspect for images by default, Shift frees it). Drag the body of a floated object = move (set inline left/top). Drag the rotate grip = free rotate (set transform: rotate(deg)). Esc / click elsewhere deselects.
   - Multi-select with Shift+click; store selected set in WC.Arrange.selection (array). Ribbon Arrange group buttons read this set.

2) Flyout menus for Position / Wrap Text / Bring Forward / Send Backward / Align / Group / Rotate — reuse WC.flyout + WC.flyItem; Position uses a 3x3 clickable grid cell (like the existing bullet/themes grids in commands.js). Split buttons (Bring Forward/Send Backward) already route through Commands.dropdown.

3) Selection Pane task pane: a right-docked panel (new div#selection-pane styled like nav/find panes already in dialogs.js/editor.css). Header "Selection" with Show All / Hide All buttons; a list where each row = object name (editable on double-click) + eye-toggle icon (visibility) + up/down reorder arrows (z-order). Clicking a row selects that object in the canvas; toggling the eye sets the object's visibility:hidden and a data-hidden flag.

CSS additions (styles/editor.css): .wc-selected outline; .wc-obj-overlay + .wc-handle (8px white squares, blue border) + .wc-rotate-grip; wrap classes .wc-wrap-square/.wc-wrap-tight/.wc-wrap-through/.wc-wrap-topbottom/.wc-wrap-behind/.wc-wrap-front/.wc-inline; #selection-pane docked panel styling.
specs.implementationPlan:
Create one new module src/renderer/js/arrange.js (window.WC.Arrange), add 8 handlers + dropdown routes in commands.js, add CSS to styles/editor.css, and register arrange.js in index.html before commands.js. No ribbon-data.js changes needed (Arrange group + all 8 cmds already defined at lines 1459-1581).

A) src/renderer/js/arrange.js — WC.Arrange:
   - State: selection = [] (selected object elements), overlay DOM, drag state.
   - init(): delegate mousedown on WC.Editor.node — if e.target.closest('.wc-shape, img') inside #editor, call select(obj, shiftKey ? additive : replace); else deselect(). Document Esc deselects; Delete/Backspace removes selected objects when not editing text. Reposition overlay on scroll/resize; hook into WC.Editor.repaginate (wrap it or add a post-repaginate callback) so the overlay follows.
   - select(obj, additive)/deselect()/isSelected(obj): manage .wc-selected class + selection array; build/refresh overlay (8 handles + rotate grip) sized to obj.getBoundingClientRect() mapped into editor coordinate space accounting for WC.Editor.zoom.
   - Drag: handle-drag resizes (mutate obj.style.width/height px; preserve aspect on corner handles for img unless Shift); body-drag moves a floated/absolute object (style.left/top); rotate-grip-drag sets data-rotate deg and obj.style.transform = rotate(deg) composed with flips — keep flip state in data-flip-x/data-flip-y so flips compose with rotation.
   - Command methods called by handlers:
     * setWrap(mode): clear wc-wrap-* classes, add the right one. Map: 'inline' -> .wc-inline (static, no float, text char); 'square'/'tight'/'through' -> float:left (or right per Position) + shape-outside (tight/through approximate contour via shape-outside) + margins; 'topBottom' -> display:block, float:none, clear:both; 'behind' -> position:absolute, z-index:-1; 'front' -> position:absolute, z-index:10. Store mode in obj.dataset.wrap.
     * setPosition(cell): cell in {tl,tc,tr,ml,mc,mr,bl,bc,br} or 'inline'. inline -> setWrap('inline'). Grid cell -> set position:absolute and left/top from the page box (left 0/50%/100% with translateX, top similarly) so it pins; default wrap to square if currently inline. 'More Layout Options...' opens WC.dialog with horizontal/vertical alignment + absolute position fields writing the same inline styles.
     * bringForward()/bringToFront()/sendBackward()/sendToBack()/inFrontOfText()/behindText(): read/modify obj.style.zIndex; to-front/back scan all objects for max/min z; inFrontOfText sets z>=10 + position:relative; behindText sets z:-1.
     * align(kind, ref): ref in {'page','margin','selected'}. Single object -> set inline left/top flush to page/margin edge or centered (read --page-w/--page-h/--page-margin via getComputedStyle on documentElement). Multiple + ref 'selected' -> compute group bounding extent, move each to align edges/centers; Distribute H/V -> sort by center, even gaps.
     * group()/ungroup()/regroup(): group wraps selection in span.wc-group (contenteditable=false, display:inline-block, position:relative); must share a common parent; move all selected in preserving relative offsets. ungroup unwraps a .wc-group restoring children. regroup re-wraps the last ungrouped member set (store ids in a module var).
     * rotate(kind): 'right90'/'left90' add/subtract 90 from data-rotate; 'flipH'/'flipV' toggle data-flip-x/data-flip-y; rebuild transform. 'more' opens WC.dialog with a numeric degrees input.
   - Selection Pane: togglePane()/refreshPane(). Build/toggle #selection-pane docked right. List every .wc-shape, img, .wc-group, .wc-wordart in document order (name from data-shape / 'Picture N' / 'Group N', persisted in dataset.objName). Row: name (dblclick rename), eye toggle (obj.style.visibility + dataset.hidden), up/down arrows (reorder z/DOM). Header Show All / Hide All. Row click -> select(obj). Refresh on selection change + document mutation.

B) src/renderer/js/commands.js — add handlers (object-targeted) near the Layout section:
   - const A = () => WC.Arrange; const sel1 = () => A().selection[0];
   - requireObj(): if no selected object, WC.toast('Select a shape or picture first.') and return false.
   - H.position = (c,node) => positionMenu(node);
   - H.wrapText = (c,node) => wrapTextMenu(node);
   - H.bringForward = () => A().bringForward();  H.sendBackward = () => A().sendBackward();
   - H.selectionPane = () => A().togglePane();
   - H.align = (c,node) => alignMenu(node);
   - H.group = (c,node) => groupMenu(node);
   - H.rotate = (c,node) => rotateMenu(node);
   Add Commands.dropdown routes: position->positionMenu, wrapText->wrapTextMenu, bringForward->forwardMenu (Bring Forward/Bring to Front/Bring in Front of Text), sendBackward->backwardMenu (Send Backward/Send to Back/Send Behind Text), align->alignMenu, group->groupMenu, rotate->rotateMenu. Menus use WC.flyout/flyItem; positionMenu's 3x3 grid built like the existing bulletMenu grid.

C) styles/editor.css — append: .wc-selected{outline:1px solid var(--word-blue);outline-offset:2px}; .wc-obj-overlay{position:absolute;pointer-events:none;z-index:50;border:1px solid var(--word-blue)}; .wc-handle{position:absolute;width:8px;height:8px;background:#fff;border:1px solid var(--word-blue);pointer-events:auto;border-radius:1px} + per-handle cursors; .wc-rotate-grip{round, cursor:grab}; wrap classes (.wc-wrap-square{float:left;shape-outside:margin-box;margin:6px 12px 6px 0}, tight/through similar, .wc-wrap-topbottom{display:block;float:none;clear:both;margin:6px auto}, .wc-wrap-behind{position:absolute;z-index:-1}, .wc-wrap-front{position:absolute;z-index:10}, .wc-inline{display:inline-block;float:none;position:static}); #selection-pane docked-right panel mirroring the existing nav/find pane look.

D) index.html — add <script src="js/arrange.js"></script> between table-tools.js and commands.js. Call WC.Arrange.init() from app.js init alongside the other module inits.
specs.testPlan:
Manual (run the Electron app via the run skill) plus DOM assertions:
1) Insert a shape (Insert > Shapes) and an image (Insert > Pictures). Click the shape: blue overlay with 8 handles + rotate grip appears; .wc-selected set. Click empty doc: overlay disappears.
2) Position > Middle Center: shape pins to page center (inline left/top set, dataset.wrap square/floated). Position > In Line with Text: object returns to inline flow (.wc-inline, no float).
3) Wrap Text > Square: text flows around the floated object (float:left + shape-outside). Behind Text: object z below text (class wc-wrap-behind, computed zIndex negative). In Front of Text: above text. Top and Bottom: text only above/below.
4) Insert two overlapping shapes. Select front, Send to Back -> zIndex < other; Bring to Front -> highest. Bring Forward / Send Backward step one level (assert numeric zIndex deltas).
5) Selection Pane: click Selection Pane -> #selection-pane lists every object with names; toggle an eye -> visibility:hidden + dataset.hidden='1'; toggle again -> visible. Double-click name -> rename persists in dataset.objName. Click a row -> object selected (overlay moves). Reorder arrows change z/DOM order. Show All/Hide All affect all rows.
6) Align: one floated object, Align Center (Align to Page) horizontally centers; Align Right flushes to right margin. Shift-select two objects, Align Selected Objects + Align Top -> top edges match; Distribute Horizontally -> equal gaps (assert computed left coords).
7) Group: Shift-select two objects, Group -> .wc-group wraps both; overlay selects the group as one. Ungroup -> children restored. Regroup -> re-wraps same members.
8) Rotate: Rotate Right 90 -> transform has rotate(90deg), data-rotate='90'; Rotate Left 90 from 90 -> 0. Flip Horizontal -> scaleX(-1) composes with rotation; Flip Vertical -> scaleY(-1). More Rotation Options -> dialog accepts a degree value and applies it.
9) Regression: typing/selection in #editor still works; repagination, header/footer, draw-tools select, and PDF export unaffected (overlay/selection-pane stripped or ignored on getHTML save like existing .pagebreak-guide handling). Resize/zoom -> overlay re-tracks the object.
10) No-selection guards: Align/Wrap/Rotate with nothing selected show the 'Select a shape or picture first' toast instead of throwing.
specs.feasibility:
Highly feasible — all eight Arrange commands map to real, working DOM/CSS behavior with the existing primitives, and the ribbon controls + cmd ids already exist in ribbon-data.js (lines 1459-1581) currently falling through to notImplemented (commands.js has no position/wrapText/etc. handlers). The objects to manipulate already exist as .wc-shape spans (contenteditable=false) and #editor img, and editor.css already gives .wc-shape a hover outline (lines 167-168), so a selection overlay is a natural extension. z-order (z-index), rotate/flip (CSS transform), wrap (float + shape-outside + position/z-index), position/align (inline left/top against the --page-w/--page-h/--page-margin CSS vars), and group/ungroup (wrapper span) are all straightforward DOM operations. The infra (WC.flyout/flyItem/flyHeader/flySep for menus, WC.dialog for More Layout/Rotation Options, WC.toast for guards/stubs, WC.el for overlay+pane, WC.icon for pane icons) is all present and used elsewhere. New file: src/renderer/js/arrange.js. Edited: src/renderer/js/commands.js, src/renderer/styles/editor.css, src/renderer/index.html (script tag), src/renderer/js/app.js (WC.Arrange.init() call). No ribbon-data.js change required.

Caveats / honest approximations (consistent with the clone's NOT_IMPLEMENTED philosophy):
- contenteditable + floated/absolute objects in a single continuous #editor: true Word wrapping across the multi-page continuous layout is only approximated by CSS float/shape-outside; Behind/In Front of Text via z-index works, but absolute positioning is relative to the editor box, not a true per-page anchor, so the 'pin to page' presets are approximate near page boundaries.
- Tight vs Through render the same (no real silhouette contour computed); 'Edit Wrap Points' is a toast stub. Move with Text / Fix Position on Page are stored as data flags but behave the same given the simplified layout.
- Rotated objects' resize handles use the unrotated bounding box (overlay doesn't counter-rotate) — acceptable approximation.
- 'More Layout Options' / 'More Rotation Options' implement the essential fields (alignment, absolute position, exact degrees), not the full multi-tab Advanced Layout dialog.
These limitations are cosmetic/edge-case; the core selection layer and all eight commands produce real, observable behavior.
apis: WC.dialog (More Layout Options, More Rotation Options)
WC.toast / WC.notImplemented (no-selection guards, Edit Wrap Points stub)
WC.Commands.run / WC.Commands.dropdown (ribbon dispatch for the 8 cmds)
CSS vars --page-w / --page-h / --page-margin (read via getComputedStyle for Position/Align)
direct DOM styling (style.zIndex, style.transform, style.left/top, float, shape-outside) — no execCommand
